response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ is not his primary language — his only substantive C/C++ work was a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, both over a decade ago. The role demands deep, current C++ expertise alongside industrial-automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development practices, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), and MQTT — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His strengths lie in TypeScript/Node.js web platforms, event-driven architectures, and DLang high-performance services, which do not transfer meaningfully to this embedded/industrial C++ context.
strengths: Broad systems experience including high-performance, low-latency service development at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems design through GISCollective and prior roles
Long track record of software craftsmanship, TDD, and mentoring — aligns with quality and documentation expectations
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated — last substantive use was 2014–2015; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
Zero evidence of industrial automation, IoT, or any relevant domain knowledge (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No exposure to Secure Development Lifecycle, IEC 62443, or any industrial cybersecurity framework
